samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement traffic_light according to the given description in the spec.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Request signal for allowing vehicles to pass.</input>
        <output id="clock" width_description="[7:0]">An 8-bit output representing the count value of the internal counter.</output>
        <output id="red">Output signal representing the state of the red traffic light.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Output signal representing the state of the yellow traffic light.</output>
        <output id="green">Output signal representing the state of the green traffic light.</output>
    </ports>
    
    <parameter_description>
        <enumeration type="state">
            <value id="idle">Initial state where all signals are set to 0. Transition to s1_red state occurs immediately.</value>
            <value id="s1_red">Sets the red signal to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to s3_green state. Otherwise, it remains in s1_red state.</value>
            <value id="s2_yellow">Sets the yellow signal to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to s1_red state. Otherwise, it remains in s2_yellow state.</value>
            <value id="s3_green">Sets the green signal to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to s2_yellow state. Otherwise, it remains in s3_green state.</value>
        </enumeration>
        
    </parameter_description>
    
    
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <register id="cnt" width="8">Internal counter for timing purposes.</register>
        <register id="state" width="2">Register representing the current state of the traffic light controller.</register>
        <wire id="red_next" width="1"/> <!-- Wire for storing next value of red signal -->
        <wire id="yellow_next" width="1"/> <!-- Wire for storing next value of yellow signal -->
        <wire id="green_next" width="1"/> <!-- Wire for storing next value of green signal -->
        
        <register id="p_red" width="1">Previous value of red signal.</register>
        <register id="p_yellow" width="1">Previous value of yellow signal.</register>
        <register id="p_green" width="1">Previous value of green signal.</register>
        
        // Assigning the next values of red, yellow, and green signals
        assign red_next = p_red;
        assign yellow_next = p_yellow;
        assign green_next = p_green;
        
        // First always block - State transition logic
        always @(posedge clk, negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset condition
                p_red <= 0;
                p_yellow <= 0;
                p_green <= 0;
                
                state <= idle;
            end else begin
                // Case statement for state transitions
                case(state)
                    idle : begin
                        p_red <= 0;
                        p_yellow <= 0;
                        p_green <= 0;
                        
                        state <= s1_red;
                    end
                    
                    s1_red : begin
                        p_red <= 1;
                        p_yellow <= 0;
                        p_green <= 0;
                        
                        if(cnt == 3)begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                        end
                        
                    end
                    
                    s2_yellow : begin
                        p_red <= 0;
                        p_yellow <= 1;
                        p_green <= 0;
                        
                        if(cnt == 3)begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                        end
                        
                    end
                    
                    s3_green : begin
                        p_red <= 0;
                        p_yellow <= 0;
                        p_green <= 1;
                        
                        if(cnt == 3)begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                        end
                        
                    end
                    
                    
                endcase
                
                
                
            end
            
            
            
            
        end
        
        
        // Second always block - Counting logic
        always@(posedge clk,negedge rst_n)begin
            if(!rst_n)begin
                cnt <= 10;
            end else begin
                if(pass_request && green)begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else begin
                    if(!green && p_green)begin
                        cnt <= 60;
                    end else if(!yellow && p_yellow)begin
                        cnt <= 5;
                    end else if(!red && p_red)begin
                        cnt <= 10;
                    end else begin
                        cnt <= cnt - 1;
                    end
                    
                end
                
                
            end
            
            
            
            
            
        end
        
        // Third always block - Output assignment
        always@(posedge clk, negedge rst_n)begin
            if(!rst_n)begin
                red <= 0;
                yellow <= 0;
                green <= 0;
            end else begin
                red <= p_red;
                yellow <= p_yellow;
                green <= p_green;
            end
            
            
        end
        
        
    </logic_description>
    
    
    
    
    <implementation>Implement traffic_light according to the given description in the spec.</implementation>
</module>
